description: 'nuri' is a course of events or changes that returns to a comparable starting condition and may begin again. The return need not reproduce every detail. 'telui' (rhythm) is perceived recurrence, 'kaero' (phase) is one segment, and 'nuwoe' (stage) belongs to a directed progression.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and carry nasal airflow into stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge, then move forward and unround into high-front 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The short form turns from the sustained nasal opening to a single tongue tap before the final vowel. In Phi, that compact return can serve as a memory aid for a course that comes round again.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A cycle makes recurrence available for reflection without pretending that each return is identical to the last.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Seasonal, lunar, agricultural, and generational cycles have long organised attention and practical work. Cyclical understandings also matter in many Indigenous knowledge traditions, whose particular cycles, obligations, and meanings belong to specific peoples and places rather than to one general system.

semantic_domains.nature: recurring courses in living and environmental systems
semantic_domains.temporal: an ordered course that returns to a comparable beginning
